pain_points: SAP IS-U end-of-maintenance 2027 forces a platform decision while day-to-day IT operations cannot be disrupted
NIS2 transposition deadline (October 2026) requires demonstrable cloud security posture across all KRITIS-classified systems
Fachkraeftemangel in cloud-native skills makes it impossible to staff hyperscaler operations teams internally
Legacy on-premises infrastructure drives 60%+ of IT budget to run-the-business, leaving insufficient capital for grid digitalization
Board pressure to reduce IT cost-per-customer-served by 30% within 2 years while maintaining 99.95% availability SLAs
buying_criteria: BSI C5 Type 2 attestation mandatory for all cloud infrastructure
Proven SAP IS-U migration references in German energy sector
Rahmenvertrag-compatible commercial model with transparent unit pricing
24/7 managed operations with guaranteed 15-minute incident response SLA
Data residency within EU with contractual sovereignty guarantees
information_sources: Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cloud Infrastructure
Peer exchange within BDEW Arbeitskreis Digitalisierung
profiles.decision_role: Economic buyer — owns the IT budget and signs off on strategic vendor decisions above EUR 1M. Champions the cloud transformation business case to the Vorstand.

pain_points: BSI KRITIS audits require evidence of cloud security controls that legacy on-premises environments cannot produce efficiently
NIS2 Article 21 mandates supply chain security assessments for all critical infrastructure IT service providers by October 2026
OT/IT convergence exposes SCADA and Netzleitstelle systems to attack surfaces that were previously air-gapped
Increasing ransomware attacks targeting German energy utilities demand zero-trust architectures but existing perimeter security cannot be dismantled overnight
buying_criteria: BSI C5 Type 2 attestation plus ISO 27001 certification for all environments
Air-gap isolation capability for OT workloads with auditable evidence
NIS2-compliant supply chain security documentation and incident reporting SLAs
Penetration testing and vulnerability management included in managed services scope
German-speaking security operations center with BSI-certified analysts
information_sources: VDE Fachkreis Cybersecurity in der Energiewirtschaft
profiles.decision_role: Veto-holder — must approve all cloud infrastructure from a security and compliance perspective. Can block or delay any vendor selection that fails BSI or NIS2 requirements. Reports directly to Vorstand.

pain_points: SektVO procurement regulations require formal tendering processes for IT contracts above EUR 431K, adding 3-6 months to vendor selection
Existing IT vendor lock-in with long-term contracts makes competitive rebidding complex and politically sensitive
Board demands cost transparency but cloud consumption models are difficult to benchmark against legacy fixed-cost contracts
Compliance with EU-Vergaberecht for public-adjacent utilities requires extensive documentation that slows procurement cycles
buying_criteria: SektVO-compliant procurement documentation and tender-ready pricing structures
Rahmenvertrag model with fixed unit prices and volume-based discount tiers
Minimum 3 comparable reference customers in German energy sector with verifiable outcomes
Exit clause and data portability guarantees to prevent vendor lock-in
Subcontractor transparency — full disclosure of fourth-party providers and data processing locations
information_sources: Peer networks within BDEW procurement roundtables
profiles.decision_role: Gatekeeper — controls the formal procurement process and vendor qualification. Can delay or restart selection if procurement compliance is not met. Must validate commercial terms before contract signature.

profiles.role: Bereichsleiter Netzbetrieb / Head of Grid Operations
pain_points: Grid digitalization (Smart Meter Rollout, Messstellenbetrieb) requires cloud-native platforms but OT teams lack cloud expertise
Real-time grid balancing systems demand sub-second latency that public cloud alone cannot guarantee without hybrid architecture
Increasing distributed energy resources (solar, wind, storage) multiply data volumes that legacy Netzleitstelle systems cannot process
Regulierungsbehörde (BNetzA) mandates digitalized grid monitoring by 2028, creating a hard deadline for infrastructure modernization
buying_criteria: Proven hybrid cloud architecture for OT/IT integration with energy sector references
Sub-100ms latency guarantees for grid control system workloads
Integration capability with existing SCADA, OMS, and Netzleitstelle systems
German-language operational support team familiar with energy grid terminology
Scalable platform for ingesting 10x current IoT data volumes from smart grid devices
profiles.decision_role: Technical evaluator and internal champion for grid-specific use cases. Drives requirements for OT/IT convergence workloads. Influences CIO on infrastructure choices that affect grid operations. Does not control IT budget but has effective veto on technology decisions affecting grid reliability.

buying_committee.size: 6-10 stakeholders across IT, security, procurement, and operations
buying_committee.decision_model: Consensus with Vorstand final approval — CIO sponsors, CISO validates security, Einkauf ensures procurement compliance, Netzbetrieb confirms operational requirements. All four must align before Vorstand presentation.
buying_committee.typical_champion: CIO / Bereichsleiter IT initiates the cloud transformation agenda and builds the business case
deal_stall_points: BSI C5 audit verification — 3-6 months if attestation gaps are found
SektVO tender process — formal procurement adds 3-6 months minimum
Betriebsrat consultation required if outsourcing affects IT staff positions
Vorstand budget approval cycle — major IT investments reviewed quarterly
NIS2 supply chain assessment — new requirement may delay vendor onboarding by 2-4 months
Proof of concept phase — large utilities typically require 3-month PoC before commitment
buying_committee.typical_deal_size: EUR 5-15M ACV for comprehensive cloud transformation programs
buying_committee.typical_sales_cycle: 12-18 months from first meeting to contract signature
